title: The Incentive

jokes.text: Kevin suddenly speaking in caveman-like broken English: 'Me doing now! Go. Stop worry.'
jokes.setup: Normal workplace conversation about paperwork
jokes.punchline: Kevin inexplicably adopts primitive speech patterns
jokes.explanation: Strong character-specific absurdist humor. Kevin's regression is unexpected and perfectly in character for his deteriorating competence over the seasons.

jokes.text: Kevin's physical description: 'Me feel good. Body strong. Sleep big last night.'
jokes.setup: Coworkers expressing concern for Kevin's wellbeing
jokes.punchline: Kevin's caveman response with 'sleep big' construction
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag with efficient, quotable phrasing. 'Sleep big' is particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Oscar's pretentious interpretation: 'It's an ironic comment on our expectations of him. A funhouse image of our model of Kevin.'
jokes.setup: Kevin clearly regressing intellectually
jokes.punchline: Oscar overthinking it as high-concept performance art
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar character moment - his need to intellectualize everything even when Kevin is obviously just being dumb.

jokes.text: Kevin's mechanic analogy: 'Me mechanic not speak English. But he know what me mean when me say, "car no go," and we best friends.'
jokes.setup: Kevin trying to justify his simplified speech
jokes.punchline: Bizarre mechanic friendship story with broken English
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic from Kevin. The mechanic friendship is completely random but feels authentic to his thought process.

jokes.text: Kevin's philosophy: 'Why waste time say lot word when few word do trick?'
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining his linguistic regression
jokes.punchline: Accidentally profound statement delivered in broken English
jokes.explanation: One of the episode's best jokes. Perfect structure, maximum economy, became a cultural meme. Kevin accidentally creates wisdom through stupidity.

jokes.text: Jim's comparison: 'The speech equivalent to just wearing underpants.'
jokes.setup: Discussing workplace speech codes
jokes.punchline: Perfectly apt analogy for Kevin's linguistic state
jokes.explanation: Sharp analogy that crystallizes the absurdity. Good Jim moment as the rational voice.

jokes.text: Kevin's response: 'Sometimes, words you no need use but need-need for talk-talk.'
jokes.setup: Jim criticizing Kevin's speech
jokes.punchline: Kevin's increasingly mangled attempt at explanation
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag but less sharply than previous moments. The redundancy is intentionally funny but not as clever.

jokes.text: Time-saving irony: 'Does it save time, though? 'Cause we've been here for about an hour.'
jokes.setup: Kevin claiming his method saves time
jokes.punchline: Jim points out they've wasted an entire hour on this
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic observation that deflates Kevin's entire premise. Good logical comedy from Jim.

jokes.text: Kevin's time philosophy: 'Many small time make big time.'
jokes.setup: Jim questioning the time-saving aspect
jokes.punchline: Kevin's caveman wisdom about time accumulation
jokes.explanation: Another accidentally profound Kevin moment. The broken grammar makes it funnier than if properly stated.

jokes.text: Kevin's life goal: 'See world.'
jokes.setup: What will Kevin do with all his saved time?
jokes.punchline: Impossibly ambitious goal stated with caveman simplicity
jokes.explanation: Sets up the SeaWorld confusion perfectly. Maximum economy with two simple words.

jokes.text: The SeaWorld confusion: 'Kevin, are you saying "see the world" or "seaworld"?'
jokes.setup: Kevin's ambiguous 'see world' statement
jokes.punchline: Jim identifying the perfect ambiguity in Kevin's broken English
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup/payoff structure. Creates genuine ambiguity from Kevin's linguistic limitations, building to the perfect punchline.

jokes.text: Kevin's clarification: 'See world. Oceans, fish, jump. China.'
jokes.setup: Trying to clarify between 'see world' and 'SeaWorld'
jokes.punchline: Kevin's description makes it even more ambiguous
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the confusion. Kevin's attempt at clarification makes it worse, which is peak Kevin comedy.

jokes.text: Kevin's presidential ambition: 'When me president, they see. They see.'
jokes.setup: Kevin reverting to normal speech after criticism
jokes.punchline: Ominous political threat delivered in caveman English
jokes.explanation: Great button to the cold open. The threatening tone with broken English is perfectly absurd Kevin.

jokes.text: The Sabre Pyramid tablet introduction
jokes.setup: Introducing a new tablet product
jokes.punchline: It's pyramid-shaped and called 'The Pyramid'
jokes.explanation: Standard absurd product gag. Functional but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Marketing slogan: 'unleash the power of the pyramid'
jokes.setup: Why is it pyramid-shaped?
jokes.punchline: Generic mystical marketing speak about pyramid power
jokes.explanation: Predictable corporate speak parody. Gets the job done but lacks originality.

jokes.text: Memory measurement confusion: '50 L' and 'How many L to a K?'
jokes.setup: Asking about memory capacity
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical unit of measurement
jokes.explanation: Good tech parody moment. The fake units are absurd enough to be funny without being too over-the-top.

jokes.text: Andy interrupting for tie advice during important meeting
jokes.setup: Dwight conducting a product meeting
jokes.punchline: New boss Andy completely derails it for fashion advice
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Andy's misplaced priorities but not particularly sharp or memorable.

jokes.text: Andy's nickname system: 'C-span' for Oscar
jokes.setup: Andy giving everyone nicknames
jokes.punchline: Calls Oscar 'C-span' (Cocker spaniel)
jokes.explanation: Mildly clever wordplay but the dog theme feels arbitrary. Shows Andy's try-hard nature.

jokes.text: Explaining the nickname: 'Cocker spaniel. Spaniel 'cause of your Spanish bloodline, cocker 'cause...'
jokes.setup: Oscar asking about his nickname
jokes.punchline: Andy trails off awkwardly at the offensive implication
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Andy's casual insensitivity. The trailing off makes it appropriately uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Dwight's strategy: 'I'm going to not care. And I'm gonna sit around quietly waiting for Andy's inevitable demise.'
jokes.setup: Andy taking over and derailing meetings
jokes.punchline: Dwight's coldly calculated response to being displaced
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment. His clinical approach to workplace revenge is very in character and darkly funny.

jokes.text: Andy's Neil Patrick Harris reference confusion
jokes.setup: Robert mentioning Neil Patrick Harris
jokes.punchline: Andy acts like NPH is his personal friend
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment showing Andy's tendency to oversell connections. Not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: Kevin quoting Andy calling his ex-wife a [bleep] and saying he [bleep] hated her guts
jokes.setup: Andy claiming to get along with his ex-wife now
jokes.punchline: Kevin loudly quoting Andy's previous harsh words about her
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment exposing Andy's hypocrisy. The bleeping adds to the awkwardness appropriately.

jokes.text: Andy's song about shower sex with his ex-wife
jokes.setup: Andy saying he can't tell what he did with his ex-wife
jokes.punchline: He has to sing about their shower encounter instead
jokes.explanation: Peak Andy cringe. The singing device is perfectly in character and the oversharing is beautifully uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Andy's ice cream guessing game with Robert
jokes.setup: Robert trying to give Andy serious feedback
jokes.punchline: Andy derails with pointless ice cream guessing game
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's tendency to avoid serious conversations but goes on too long without enough payoff.

jokes.text: The cold coffee incident - Erin serving old coffee as 'cold beverage'
jokes.setup: Robert asks for a cold beverage and says coffee
jokes.punchline: Erin literally serves him cold, old coffee
jokes.explanation: Good Erin moment showing her literal-mindedness. The extended awkwardness works well.

jokes.text: Andy claiming he's 'been craving a freezing cup of old coffee' to cover for Erin
jokes.setup: Trying to save Erin from embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Obviously lying about wanting disgusting old coffee
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's protective instincts toward Erin but the lie is transparently absurd in a good way.

jokes.text: Robert's business philosophy about personal service vs superstores
jokes.setup: Explaining why small suppliers survive
jokes.punchline: Grandiose speech about an obvious business concept
jokes.explanation: Shows Robert's pretentious management style but goes on too long without enough comedic payoff.

jokes.text: The doubling sales demand and Andy's overconfidence
jokes.setup: Robert demanding doubled sales growth
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately agreeing then admitting he's kidding about the timeline
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's main plot. Andy's backpedaling is in character but not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: Jim and Pam's suggestion to talk about how children grow up fast during business meeting
jokes.setup: Andy asking for business ideas to double sales
jokes.punchline: Completely irrelevant parenting observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect non sequitur that highlights the meeting's lack of direction. Good deadpan delivery implied.

jokes.text: Jim suggesting 'Fart' as a business solution
jokes.setup: Andy asking what a solution would look like
jokes.punchline: Jim just says 'Fart' completely deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment. Maximum economy, perfectly delivered absurdist response that shows his opinion of the meeting.

jokes.text: Andy's leg falling asleep during his own meeting
jokes.setup: Andy trying to end the failed meeting on a positive note
jokes.punchline: His leg is asleep, undermining his attempt to leave gracefully
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy button to the scene but fairly predictable. Shows Andy's general incompetence.

jokes.text: Andy singing 'Rub a dub dub with the apricot scrub' while working
jokes.setup: Andy trying to appear productive
jokes.punchline: Singing random jingle-like song
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's nervous energy and tendency toward musical outbursts but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Darryl's sexual innuendo about barely being able to walk
jokes.setup: Darryl meeting with his ex-wife at work
jokes.punchline: Loud sexual boasting about their encounter
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual oversharing joke. Gets the cringe but relies on obvious innuendo without much craft.

jokes.text: Dwight's beet farm struggles: 'We made some inroads in salad, but heirloom tomatoes are pushing back'
jokes.setup: Discussing the farm's business challenges
jokes.punchline: Treating vegetables like warring factions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment treating agriculture like military strategy. The personification of vegetables is absurdist gold.

jokes.text: Pam's parenting magazine obsession: 'This is Cece's favorite magazine. She loves the pictures of babies. She looks at it when she's on the potty'
jokes.setup: Jim asking if Pam has read the parenting magazine
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's actually for their toddler's bathroom entertainment
jokes.explanation: Nice parenting reality moment. The detail about potty time is relatable and mildly funny.

jokes.text: Andy's incentive prize reveal under the blanket
jokes.setup: Mysterious blanket-covered prizes
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous collection of random items including a vibrator
jokes.explanation: Sets up the incentive plot but the random prize selection feels lazy rather than cleverly absurd.

jokes.text: Oscar asking 'Is that a vibrator?' about the prizes
jokes.setup: Looking at Andy's incentive prizes
jokes.punchline: Directly addressing the inappropriate item
jokes.explanation: Predictable response to an obvious setup. Gets the awkwardness but no real craft involved.

jokes.text: Andy's escalating tattoo promises: dress (500 points), naked run (1,000 points), ass tattoo (5,000 points)
jokes.setup: Staff wanting better incentives than cheap prizes
jokes.punchline: Andy promising increasingly extreme personal humiliation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure building to the episode's main premise. Each level is progressively more absurd and Andy-appropriate.

jokes.text: Doughnut on dingdong description for the 1,000 point reward
jokes.setup: Describing the naked parking lot run
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd detail about the doughnut placement
jokes.explanation: The specific absurd detail makes it memorable. Andy's childish terminology adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: 'The stern of the old SS Bernard' - Andy referring to his butt
jokes.setup: Describing where the tattoo would go
jokes.punchline: Nautical metaphor for his buttocks using his own name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy line combining nautical pretension with crude humor. The self-reference makes it perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Stanley's motivation transformation from naptime to active work
jokes.setup: Jim describing Stanley's usual work style
jokes.punchline: Stanley now actively working because of the tattoo incentive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley character moment. The idea that only Andy's ass tattoo could motivate him is peak character comedy.

jokes.text: Jim's big sale netting 120 points while claiming not to care about points
jokes.setup: Jim saying he doesn't care about the point system
jokes.punchline: Making a huge sale and wanting a point receipt anyway
jokes.explanation: Good Jim moment showing even he's been drawn in by the incentive despite his usual detachment.

jokes.text: Andy calling his business school professor about 'classic ass-tattoo incentive situations'
jokes.setup: Andy desperately seeking management advice
jokes.punchline: Treating his specific bizarre situation like a common business scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor. The phrase 'classic ass-tattoo incentive situations' is perfectly ridiculous and quotable.

jokes.text: Tattoo design: 'I'm not as think as you drunk I am'
jokes.setup: Choosing what to tattoo on Andy
jokes.punchline: Classic drunk person saying with mixed-up words
jokes.explanation: Old joke that everyone knows. Gets a chuckle but lacks originality or cleverness for a major plot point.

jokes.text: Alternative tattoo: 'Do not resuscitate'
jokes.setup: Choosing tattoo text for Andy's butt
jokes.punchline: Medical directive in inappropriate location
jokes.explanation: Darker humor that works well. The medical context on someone's butt is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Phyllis's baby coming out of butt tattoo design suggestion
jokes.setup: Phyllis suggesting a tattoo design
jokes.punchline: Anatomically inappropriate birth imagery
jokes.explanation: Appropriately gross and uncomfortable suggestion from Phyllis. Gets the cringe factor but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Andy's realization: 'But my ass is only so big. I mean, I can't do this every day.'
jokes.setup: Andy realizing his incentive system worked too well
jokes.punchline: Practical limitation of using body parts as rewards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic. The practical consideration of limited body real estate is absurdly logical.

jokes.text: Andy's declaration: 'Let's ink my stink!'
jokes.setup: Andy psyching himself up for the tattoo
jokes.punchline: Rhyming euphemism for getting his butt tattooed
jokes.explanation: Peak Andy catchphrase. The rhyme and euphemism are perfectly in character and memorable.

jokes.text: Andy's philosophy: 'My heart belongs to music. But my ass belongs to these people.'
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his commitment to the staff
jokes.punchline: Romantic sentiment undercut by crude anatomical reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent Andy line. Perfect juxtaposition of noble sentiment with crude reality. Highly quotable and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Andy's reaction to cotton swab: 'That was just a cotton swab.' 'Invest in softer cotton, sir.'
jokes.setup: Andy screaming in pain during tattoo prep
jokes.punchline: It was just the cleaning, and Andy gives business advice
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Andy's characteristic business-speak even while in pain.

jokes.text: Robert's analysis: 'Because he's all surface, uncomplicated. What you see is what you get.'
jokes.setup: Why Robert chose Andy to run the office
jokes.punchline: Describing Andy's shallow nature as a management asset
jokes.explanation: Insightful character observation that's both insulting and weirdly complimentary to Andy.

jokes.text: Final tattoo reveal: 'It's a nard dog.'
jokes.setup: Everyone expecting text on Andy's butt
jokes.punchline: It's actually a picture of a dog representing his nickname
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the nickname theme and subverts expectation of text. Perfectly Andy-appropriate final image.

jokes.text: Robert's final observation: 'There's something about an underdog that really inspires the unexceptional.'
jokes.setup: Watching Andy's success with the staff
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment calling the entire staff unexceptional
jokes.explanation: Perfect Robert line - simultaneously insightful and insulting to everyone involved. Great button to the episode.

jokes.text: Angela calling social services on Pam for drinking herbal tea while pregnant
jokes.setup: Angela discussing an 'ethical dilemma' about a coworker
jokes.punchline: She already called social services over herbal tea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela moment showing her judgmental nature and tendency to take extreme action over minor issues.

tmdb_overview: After Robert California challenges the Dunder Mifflin staffers to double their sales, Andy, taking matters into his own hands, creates an incentive program. Eventually, he promises to tattoo himself if the staff doubles their sales. Meanwhile, Pam strikes up an unlikely friendship with Angela, both of whom are pregnant.
